name: Stepper
description: A stepper guides an operator through a task in ordered steps — onboarding a new vehicle, building a report, running a bulk assignment. It shows progress and what's left to do. Stepper itself is the outer shell; each step is composed as a real @mui/material/Step child, typically containing a StepLabel (icon + label).
keyRule: Use a stepper for a sequence that benefits from being broken up and tracked. For peer views with no order, use Tabs instead.
correction: 1:1 name match with the old component, but the old Stepper.tsx invented a `steps: StepperStep[]` array prop and derived each step's completed/active/upcoming/error state itself, mirroring a custom Cartrack `.stepper2` markup because MDC Web has no stock stepper component. The real @mui/material/Stepper takes ordinary React `children` (one Step per step) and drives active/completed state via its own `activeStep` index passed through context — there is no `steps` array prop and no per-step 'error' state on Stepper or Step (error styling is StepLabel's own `error` prop).

variants.name: Horizontal
variants.whenToUse: A few steps with short labels; fits across the top. Default orientation.

variants.name: Vertical
variants.whenToUse: Steps with more content; pair with StepContent, which expands under each active step. Set orientation="vertical".

variants.name: Linear
variants.whenToUse: Steps must be completed in order. Default (`nonLinear` is false).

variants.name: Non-linear
variants.whenToUse: Operators may jump between steps freely. Set `nonLinear`, and make each Step's header a StepButton so it's clickable.

variants.name: Alternative label
variants.whenToUse: Horizontal stepper where the label sits under the icon rather than beside it. Set `alternativeLabel`.

anatomy.name: Stepper (root)
anatomy.description: the outer container; owns `activeStep`, `orientation`, `nonLinear`, `alternativeLabel`, and the `connector` used between steps.

anatomy.name: Step (sub-component, per step)
anatomy.description: @mui/material/Step — one required per step, rendered as a Stepper child. Accepts `completed`, `active` (usually left to auto-derive from Stepper's activeStep), `disabled`, and `index`/`last` (managed internally).

anatomy.name: StepLabel (sub-component)
anatomy.description: @mui/material/StepLabel — the icon + label (+ optional secondary/subtext via `optional`) inside a Step. Its `error` prop is the real way to show an error state on a step; Stepper/Step have no separate error prop.

anatomy.name: StepConnector (sub-component)
anatomy.description: the line linking steps; default rendering, replaceable via Stepper's `connector` prop.

anatomy.name: StepContent (sub-component, vertical only)
anatomy.description: the expandable body shown under the active step in a vertical stepper — holds the fields/actions for that step.

anatomy.name: StepButton (sub-component, optional)
anatomy.description: a clickable step header, used instead of a plain StepLabel to support non-linear navigation.

anatomy.name: Navigation (external)
anatomy.description: Back / Next / Finish controls are not part of Stepper at all — build them yourself (e.g. plain Buttons) and drive `activeStep` from your own state.

behaviors.name: Progress via activeStep
behaviors.description: Stepper passes its `activeStep` index down through context; each Step compares its own position to it to render as completed (index < activeStep), active (index === activeStep), or upcoming (index > activeStep) — unless a Step explicitly overrides `completed`/`active` itself.

behaviors.name: Validation (not built in)
behaviors.description: Stepper has no validation logic. In a linear flow, the consumer must decide when it's valid to increment `activeStep`, and surface errors via StepLabel's `error` prop on the affected step.

behaviors.name: Back/skip (not built in)
behaviors.description: Going back, skipping optional steps, and Finish behavior are all consumer-built — Stepper only tracks and displays which step is active; it exposes no Back/Next/Skip API.

states.treatment: Primary-coloured StepIcon with a check mark (auto-derived from activeStep, or explicit Step `completed` prop).
Primary-coloured StepIcon with the step number; bold label (a fontWeight override on top of the base label typography, not a distinct MUI variant).
Set via StepLabel's own `error` prop on the affected step — there is no Stepper- or Step-level error state.
Set per-Step via its `disabled` prop; dims that step and (with StepButton) prevents clicking it.

specs.value: ~24px circle (SVG icon component, filled/outlined per state) — an SVG shape, not a CSS border-radius element
~14px/400 base (body2), with a ~500-weight CSS override applied for the active/completed label — not a switch to a distinct Typography variant
1px line by default (StepConnector), replaceable via the `connector` prop

props.name: activeStep
props.description: Zero-based index of the current step. Default 0. Set to -1 to disable all steps.

props.name: children
props.description: Two or more @mui/material/Step components, one per step — replaces the old doc's `steps: StepperStep[]` array prop.

props.name: nonLinear
props.description: If true, Stepper won't assist in controlling steps for a linear flow — pair with StepButton for free navigation. Default false.

props.name: alternativeLabel
props.description: If true and orientation is horizontal, positions the step label under the icon instead of beside it. Default false.

props.name: connector
props.description: Element placed between each step. Default: <StepConnector />.

props.name: elevation
props.description: Historical Paper-shaped prop Stepper's types still accept for backward compatibility — the real Stepper root renders as a plain 'div' in current MUI, not a Paper, so this typically has no visible effect.

props.name: component
props.description: Overrides the root element type. Default 'div'.

doThis: Keep steps few and clearly named.
Validate before advancing activeStep in a linear flow, and surface the error on the relevant Step via StepLabel's error prop.
Build your own Back/Next controls that drive activeStep — Stepper renders state, it doesn't navigate for you.
dontDoThis: Don't use a Stepper for unordered, peer views — use Tabs.
Don't invent a `steps` array prop or per-step 'error' state on Stepper/Step (as the old doc did) — compose real Step/StepLabel children and use StepLabel's own error prop.
Don't create long, dozen-step flows; split the task.
accessibility: Convey the current step and total ("Step 2 of 3") to assistive tech — the active Step's icon/label area exposes aria-current="step" internally.
If steps are reachable via StepButton, they're real interactive elements with keyboard support and visible focus.
Set StepLabel's `error` prop (not colour alone) so an errored step is announced, and add visible error text near the step content.

related.name: LinearProgress
related.why: Indeterminate/determinate progress display, not discrete named steps
